description:
This map displays an aerial or satellite false-colour infrared view of a fragmented landscape, segmented into distinct land cover / land use (LULC) polygons, each marked with a numerical classification code. The underlying imagery shows vegetation in shades of green and red, and bare soil in reddish-brown tones.

Specific polygons are identified by 4-digit codes:
*   A large, rectangular reddish-brown field in the lower-left is labelled `2110`.
*   A central polygon, distinctly outlined in cyan, is labelled `3220`. This area features visible parallel rows of vegetation.
*   A linear strip of vegetated land in the upper-center, adjacent to a road-like feature, is labelled `3110`.
*   A small, irregular polygon in the upper-right is labelled `1113`.
*   Two patches of reddish-brown land in the lower-center and lower-left are labelled `4100`.
*   A pink-outlined polygon in the lower-right is partially visible with the label `39`.

Most polygon boundaries are delineated by orange lines, while the central `3220` polygon is highlighted with a cyan outline. Pink-coloured boundaries are also visible in the upper-right and lower-right corners of the map. No scale bar, compass, legend, or reference year is visible.
